TipMetadata
TipLog
  • 07 May. 2026 - Original PR

1. Summary

  1. Background and rationale:

  2. Methods:

  3. Interpretation: An indicator value of 1 signifies … Values decreasing toward zero reflect…

  4. Results and development needs:

2. About the underlying data

This indicator uses data from the Norwegian National Forest Inventory (NFI, landsskoginventaret). The bilberry cover by area is recorded for each NFI inventory plot. There are about 12 000 NFI plots in forest ecosystems, distributed on a regular grid, with varying grid density for forest below/above the coniferous forest line and in Finnmark county. The indicator value is an unbiased area-weighted estimate of the bilberry cover for NFI plots in any selected region. All NFI plots are sampled over a 5-year period, with a representative sampling scheme every year, allowing unbiased estimates from data for each year. For this indicator data from each full inventory cycle of 5 years are used, starting in 2010, meaning that results reported for 2024 are based on data from the years 2020-2024. The data are available upon request from NFI.

2.1 Spatial and temporal resolution and extent

Data are available from 2010, when the bilberry cover by area was first recorded based on current methods. Annual estimates are possible due to representative sampling every year. For this indicator all data from each full inventory cycle of 5 years are used, starting in 2010. The data are representative for all productiver forest land of Norway. Spatial resolution should be limited to the resolution applied to reference values, that is counties or sub-counties for scaling and regions for aggregated reporting.

2.2 Sampling design

2.3 Original units

The indicator is specified as the percentage (%) of the area covered by bilberry on the NFI plot. In the analyses, this value is converted to a proportion before area weighting, aggregation, and scaling.

2.4 Instructions for citing, using and accessing data

The data used for this indicator have been made available by the Norwegian National Forest Inventory under a specific data use agreement of 2025-10-20, between NIBIO and the specified users. Further use of these data requires a separate data user agreement with NIBIO.

2.5 Additional comments about the dataset

3. Indicator properties

3.1 Ecosystem Condition Typology Class (ECT)

3.1.1 Other condition typologies

3.2 Ecosystem condition characteristic

3.3 Collinearities with other indicators

3.4 Impact factors

3.5 Spatial aggregation

The spatial aggregation pathway is Scale - Aggregate - Truncate. For each NFI plot we first convert bilberry cover from percent to proportion and create indicator_continuous_weighted = indicator_cover * au_areal. Non-scaled indicator values for each reporting period are calculated separately for each geographic unit as area-weighted arithmetic means of all NFI plots within that unit: sum of indicator_continuous_weighted divided by sum of au_areal. This is done for counties or sub-counties, regions, and at national level. Scaling is applied only after these non-scaled county or sub-county aggregates have been calculated. Each county or sub-county is scaled against its own lower reference level X0 (0% cover) and upper reference level X100 using a linear scaling function; scaled values above 1 are truncated to 1. Regional and national scaled point estimates are not obtained by scaling a single aggregated non-scaled value with one reference level. They are area-weighted means of the already scaled county or sub-county values, using each county or sub-county’s represented area as weight. Non-scaled regional and national point estimates are still calculated directly from all plots within each region or the whole country.

4. Reference condition and levels

4.1 Reference condition

4.2 Reference levels

The lower reference level X0 is set to 0% bilberry cover and the upper reference level X100 is set separately for each county or sub-county. X0 denotes the worst possible condition and X100 denotes the best possible condition on the original cover scale. Between X0 and X100, indicator values are scaled linearly to a 0-1 indicator scale. Scaled values above 1 are truncated to 1 when bilberry cover exceeds the county-specific X100. The county and sub-county X100 values used in the analyses are listed in Section 9.4. X0 is 0 for all units.

4.2.1 Spatial resolution and validity

Reference levels are defined at county or sub-county resolution, not as one national value. Scaling is therefore applied to area-weighted non-scaled indicator values that have first been aggregated to the matching county or sub-county unit. Regional and national reporting uses area-weighted aggregation of these scaled county or sub-county values rather than one common X100 for the aggregated non-scaled value. The indicator should not be interpreted at finer spatial resolution than the county or sub-county reference levels allow.

5. Uncertainties

5.1 General uncertainties

5.2 Reported uncertainties

The uncertainties reported in Section 10 are inferential and refer to uncertainty around the point estimates, not to the full spatial variation in bilberry cover among NFI plots. For each reporting period, non-scaled indicator values are bootstrapped by resampling NFI plots with replacement within the relevant geographic unit and recalculating the area-weighted mean. This is done separately for counties or sub-counties, regions, and the national level. For scaled county or sub-county values, the bootstrap mean, median, standard error, confidence interval, and quartiles from the non-scaled bootstrap are transformed with the same county-specific linear scaling function used for the point estimate. This is an approximation when many bootstrap replicates exceed X100, because truncation is not applied inside each bootstrap replicate before summarising the scaled distribution. For scaled regional and national values, uncertainty is estimated by bootstrapping plots, calculating county or sub-county non-scaled values in each replicate, scaling each county or sub-county with its own reference levels, and then taking the area-weighted mean of the scaled county or sub-county values within each region or for the whole country. The resulting bootstrap distribution is summarised with mean, median, standard error, confidence interval, and quartiles.

6. References

7. Import and prepare datasets

The bilberry cover by area is recorded for each NFI plot in the LSK export. For this indicator we use data from each full 5-year inventory cycle, starting in 2010. Aggregations to counties, regions, or the whole country use the NFI area weight au_areal in two steps. First each plot gets a weighted contribution indicator_continuous_weighted = indicator_cover * au_areal (section 9.1). Then the area-weighted mean for each geographic unit is the sum of indicator_continuous_weighted divided by sum of au_areal within that unit (section 9.2).

The NFI sample uses denser plot spacing below the coniferous forest line and in Finnmark than elsewhere; au_areal reflects how much forest area each plot represents so that aggregated estimates aim to be representative of all productive forest land. Where a plot is surveyed in two parts, the export contains two rows (field hel_delt_flate); we retain both rows and include each in the area-weighted calculations with its own au_areal.

Reference levels for scaling (section 4) are defined for counties or sub-counties.

Load data 
# Load main data file
# This export wraps each physical line in an extra pair of double quotes, so a
# single read_csv() sees one column spanning the whole file. Read once as one
# character column per line (readr unwraps the outer quotes), then parse the
# inner CSV text.
lsk_inner_csv <- read_csv(
  here("data/LSK_data_okologisk_tilstand_2026.csv"),
  col_names = FALSE,
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8"),
  col_types = cols(.default = col_character())
) |>
  pull(1) |>
  paste(collapse = "\n")

lsk_data_raw <- read_csv(
  I(lsk_inner_csv),
  show_col_types = FALSE,
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8")
) |>
  clean_names()

8. Spatial units

Aggregated indicator values should not be applied for spatial units finer than those that allow reliable estimates, e.g., regions.

9. Analyses

9.1 Prepare data and create derived variables

This section prepares the NFI plot data for calculating the indicator. We retain both productive and unproductive forest plots (arealtype 1 and 12), and then create the derived variables used in subsequent aggregation steps.

First we define the 5-year reporting periods that match the temporal structure of the indicator.

Define reporting periods 
# Define multiple 5-year reporting periods starting from 2010
reporting_periods <- list(
  "2010-2014" = 2010:2014,
  "2015-2019" = 2015:2019,
  "2020-2024" = 2020:2024
)

Next we filter the raw dataset to include only the two relevant forest area types, remove records missing in the blaabaer_gjsn variable, standardise the geography fields. NFI bilberry cover is recorded on a 0-100 percent scale and is converted to a proportion before aggregation and scaling. Area-weighted values are created using au_areal.

Create derived variables 
lsk_data <- lsk_data_raw |>
  filter(
    # productive and unproductive forest plots
    arealtype %in% c(1, 12)
  ) |>
  # remove records with missing data on bilberry cover
  filter(!is.na(blaabaer_gjsn)) |>
  mutate(
    fylke_name = region_fylke |>
      stringr::str_remove("^\\d+-\\d+\\s+") |>
      stringr::str_remove("^\\d+\\s*") |>
      stringr::str_squish(),
    county_part = assign_bbca_county_part(fylke_name),
    fylke_name_clean = stringi::stri_trans_general(
      stringr::str_squish(
        tolower(
          stringr::str_replace_all(
            normalize_county_parts(fylke_name),
            "[0-9.-]",
            " "
          )
        )
      ),
      "Latin-ASCII"
    ),
    region_code = assign_region_code(fylke_name_clean),
    region_name = assign_region_name(region_code),

    indicator_cover = blaabaer_gjsn / 100,
    indicator_continuous_weighted = indicator_cover * au_areal
  )

Finally, we retain only the columns required for subsequent aggregation and reporting.

Select downstream fields 
lsk_data <- lsk_data |>
  dplyr::select(
    sesong,
    au_areal,
    arealtype,
    region_code,
    region_name,
    county_part,
    fylke_name,
    fylke_name_clean,
    blaabaer_gjsn,
    indicator_continuous_weighted
  )

9.2 Calculate non-scaled area-weighted indicator values

For each reporting period, we calculate non-scaled indicator values separately for counties or sub-counties, regions, and the whole country. Within each geographic unit, the point estimate is the area-weighted arithmetic mean of all NFI plots in that unit: sum of indicator_continuous_weighted divided by sum of au_areal. County or sub-county estimates use the plot-level fylke_name field; regional estimates use region_code; the national estimate uses all plots in the period.

Calculate non-scaled values 
 bbca_results <- purrr::imap(
   reporting_periods,
   ~ calculate_period_indicators(lsk_data, .y, .x)
 ) |>
   purrr::compact()
 
 bbca_results_county_all_periods   <- purrr::map_dfr(bbca_results, "county")
 bbca_results_region_all_periods   <- purrr::map_dfr(bbca_results, "region")
 bbca_results_national_all_periods <- purrr::map_dfr(bbca_results, "national")

9.3 Bootstrap draws per county

For each county and period, NFI plots are resampled with replacement 1000 times. Each replicate computes the area-weighted mean non-scaled indicator value, giving a distribution of 1000 draws per county. These raw draws will be scaled with ec_normalise() and then used to calculate county-level uncertainty and to propagate uncertainty through the spatial hierarchy.

Bootstrap draws per county (1000 per county per period) 
county_draws_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(bbca_results, function(period_result, period_name) {
  county_meta <- period_result$county |>
    dplyr::select(fylke_name, county_part, total_area, region_code)

  purrr::imap_dfr(
    split(period_result$data, period_result$data$fylke_name),
    function(county_data, county_name) {
      boot <- bootstrap_indicator(county_data, n_bootstrap = 1000)
      dplyr::tibble(
        period           = period_name,
        fylke_name       = county_name,
        sim_id           = seq_along(boot$draws),
        non_scaled_value = boot$draws
      )
    }
  ) |>
    dplyr::left_join(county_meta, by = "fylke_name")
})

9.4 Scale indicator values and aggregate to regional and national levels

County or sub-county reference values are imported manually.

Reference values by county or sub-county 
# X100 reference values (% bilberry cover by area)
bbca_reference_values <- dplyr::tibble(
  county_part = c(
    "Øs", "OA", "HeS", "HeN", "OpS", "OpN", "BuS", "BuN", "Ve",
    "TeS", "TeN", "AA", "VA", "Ro", "Ho", "SF", "MR", "ST", "NT",
    "NoS", "NoN", "Tr", "Fi"
  ),
  x100_pct = c(
    12.5, 12.5, 12.5, 10, 12.5, 10, 12.5, 10, 12.5,
    12.5, 10, 12.5, 12.5, 12.5, 12.5, 12.5, 12.5, 12.5, 12.5,
    10, 5, 5, 2.5
  )
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(x100 = x100_pct / 100)

x0 <- 0
county_reference <- bbca_reference_values |>
  dplyr::select(county_part, x100)

Each county’s 1000 non-scaled draws from section 9.3 are scaled with ecTools::ec_normalise() (linear, X0 = 0, county-specific X100). Figure 1 shows the mapping for each sub-county: the slope of each panel reflects its X100 — northern counties with low reference values (e.g. Finnmark, X100 = 2.5%) reach the maximum indicator value at much lower cover than eastern counties (e.g. Østfold, X100 = 12.5%).

Scale county draws; derive county uncertainty and feed ec_upscale 
county_draws_scaled <- county_draws_long |>
  dplyr::left_join(county_reference, by = "county_part") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(x100)) |>  # drops Buskerud V until mapping is resolved - see TODO in functions.R
  dplyr::mutate(
    scaled_value = ec_normalise(
      non_scaled_value, 
      x0 = 0, 
      x100 = x100, 
      fun = "linear"),
    nation = "National"
  )

# Summarise scaled draws per county → county-level results table (10.1)
county_scaled_with_uncertainty <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, fylke_name, county_part, total_area) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    scaled_mean     = mean(scaled_value),
    scaled_median   = median(scaled_value),
    scaled_ci_lower = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.025)),
    scaled_ci_upper = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.975)),
    scaled_q1       = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.25)),
    scaled_q3       = as.numeric(quantile(scaled_value, 0.75)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    bbca_results_county_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, fylke_name, n_plots),
    by = c("period", "fylke_name")
  )
Normalisation diagnostic figure 
norm_plot_data <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::group_by(county_part) |>
  dplyr::slice_sample(n = 100) |>
  dplyr::ungroup()

bbca_norm_plot <- ec_norm_plot(norm_plot_data$non_scaled_value, norm_plot_data$scaled_value) +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ norm_plot_data$county_part, scales = "free_x") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Bilberry cover (proportion)",
    y = "Scaled indicator value"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme(axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(size = 9))
bbca_norm_plot$layers[[1]]$aes_params$size <- 3

ggsave(
  filename = here::here("img/bbca_norm_plot.png"),
  plot     = bbca_norm_plot,
  scale    = 1.5
)
Saving 12 x 12 in image
Normalisation diagnostic figure 
bbca_norm_plot

The scaled draws are then passed to ecTools::ec_upscale(), which propagates uncertainty through the spatial hierarchy. For each of 1000 Monte Carlo iterations, one draw is sampled per county and the area-weighted mean gives one regional value; a second ec_upscale() call repeats this from regions to national level.

ec_upscale: county to region 
bbca_mc_regional <- ecTools::ec_upscale(
  data        = county_draws_scaled,
  variable    = scaled_value,
  weight      = total_area,
  start_units = fylke_name,
  end_units   = region_code,
  year        = period,
  end_units_name = "region_code",
  n = 1000
)
saveRDS(bbca_mc_regional, here::here("data/bbca_mc_regional.rds"))
ec_upscale: region to national 
# Region-level area weights (sum of county total_area within each region)
region_area_weights <- county_draws_scaled |>
  dplyr::distinct(period, fylke_name, total_area, region_code) |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(region_area = sum(total_area), .groups = "drop")

bbca_mc_regional_for_national <- bbca_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::left_join(region_area_weights, by = c("period", "region_code")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(nation = "National")

bbca_mc_national <- ecTools::ec_upscale(
  data        = bbca_mc_regional_for_national,
  variable    = sampled_mean,
  weight      = region_area,
  start_units = region_code,
  end_units   = nation,
  year        = period,
  end_units_name = "nation",
  n = 1000
)
saveRDS(bbca_mc_national, here::here("data/bbca_mc_national.rds"))
Combine regional and national MC draws; build summary tables 
bbca_mc_combined <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  bbca_mc_regional |> dplyr::select(period, region = region_code, sampled_mean),
  bbca_mc_national |> dplyr::select(period, region = nation,      sampled_mean)
)

# County point estimates (non-bootstrapped) scaled for the truncation check
county_scaled_all_periods <- bbca_results_county_all_periods |>
  dplyr::left_join(county_reference, by = "county_part") |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(x100)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    scaled_value = ec_normalise(indicator_value, x0 = x0, x100 = x100, fun = "linear")
  )

# Point-estimate summaries (median of MC draws) used in the truncation check
region_scaled_agg_all_periods <- bbca_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(scaled_value = median(sampled_mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = assign_region_name(region_code))

national_scaled_agg_all_periods <- bbca_mc_national |>
  dplyr::group_by(period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(scaled_value = median(sampled_mean), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(region = "National")

# Uncertainty summaries for the results tables (section 10)
region_scaled_agg_with_uncertainty <- bbca_mc_regional |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_code) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    scaled_mean   = mean(sampled_mean),
    scaled_median = median(sampled_mean),
    ci_lower      = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    ci_upper      = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    q1            = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.25)),
    q3            = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.75)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = assign_region_name(region_code)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    bbca_results_region_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, region_code, total_area, n_plots),
    by = c("period", "region_code")
  )

national_scaled_agg_with_uncertainty <- bbca_mc_national |>
  dplyr::group_by(period) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    scaled_mean   = mean(sampled_mean),
    scaled_median = median(sampled_mean),
    ci_lower      = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    ci_upper      = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    q1            = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.25)),
    q3            = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.75)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region = "National") |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    bbca_results_national_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, total_area, n_plots),
    by = "period"
  )

# Truncation check across all spatial levels
truncation_summary <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  county_scaled_all_periods |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      bbca_reference_values |> dplyr::select(county_part, reference_value_pct = x100_pct),
      by = "county_part"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level = "County",
      exceeds_threshold = indicator_value > x100,
      original_value_pct = round(indicator_value * 100, 2),
      region_code = NA_character_,
      region_name = NA_character_
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(period, level, region_code, region_name, fylke_name,
                  reference_value_pct, exceeds_threshold, original_value_pct),
  region_scaled_agg_all_periods |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      bbca_results_region_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, region_code, indicator_value),
      by = c("period", "region_code")
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level = "Region",
      exceeds_threshold = scaled_value >= 1,
      original_value_pct = round(indicator_value * 100, 2),
      reference_value_pct = NA_real_,
      fylke_name = NA_character_
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(period, level, region_code, region_name, fylke_name,
                  reference_value_pct, exceeds_threshold, original_value_pct),
  national_scaled_agg_all_periods |>
    dplyr::left_join(
      bbca_results_national_all_periods |> dplyr::select(period, indicator_value),
      by = "period"
    ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      level = "National",
      exceeds_threshold = scaled_value >= 1,
      original_value_pct = round(indicator_value * 100, 2),
      reference_value_pct = NA_real_,
      region_code = NA_character_,
      region_name = "National",
      fylke_name = NA_character_
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(period, level, region_code, region_name, fylke_name,
                  reference_value_pct, exceeds_threshold, original_value_pct)
)

truncation_counts <- truncation_summary |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, level) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_total = dplyr::n(),
    n_exceeding_reference = sum(exceeds_threshold, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_exceeding_reference = round(100 * n_exceeding_reference / n_total, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

truncated_values <- truncation_summary |>
  dplyr::filter(exceeds_threshold) |>
  dplyr::arrange(period, level, dplyr::desc(original_value_pct))

At county or sub-county level, truncation is evaluated by comparing the non-scaled point estimate to the matching X100. At regional and national level, the tables report cases where the ec_upscale() median equals 1. The following tables summarise how often truncation occurred in each period and list the affected units.

Report truncation 
truncation_counts |>
  dplyr::rename(
    Period = period,
    Level = level,
    `Total` = n_total,
    `Exceeding reference` = n_exceeding_reference,
    `% Exceeding reference` = pct_exceeding_reference
  ) |>
  gt::gt() |>
  gt::tab_style(
    style = gt::cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = gt::cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  gt::fmt_number(
    columns = `% Exceeding reference`,
    decimals = 1
  )
Period Level Total Exceeding reference % Exceeding reference
2010-2014 County 23 0 0.0
2010-2014 National 1 0 0.0
2010-2014 Region 5 0 0.0
2015-2019 County 23 1 4.3
2015-2019 National 1 0 0.0
2015-2019 Region 5 0 0.0
2020-2024 County 23 6 26.1
2020-2024 National 1 0 0.0
2020-2024 Region 5 0 0.0
Report truncation 
if (nrow(truncated_values) > 0) {
  truncated_values |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      location = dplyr::case_when(
        level == "County" ~ fylke_name,
        level == "Region" ~ region_name,
        level == "National" ~ "National",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      period,
      level,
      location,
      reference_value_pct,
      original_value_pct
    ) |>
    dplyr::rename(
      Period = period,
      Level = level,
      Location = location,
      `Reference (%)` = reference_value_pct,
      `Original Value (%)` = original_value_pct
    ) |>
    gt::gt() |>
    gt::tab_style(
      style = gt::cell_text(weight = "bold"),
      locations = gt::cells_column_labels()
    ) |>
    gt::fmt_number(
      columns = c(`Reference (%)`, `Original Value (%)`),
      decimals = 1
    )
} else {
  cat("\nNo values exceeded their reference and required truncation.\n")
}
Period Level Location Reference (%) Original Value (%)
2015-2019 County Telemark NV 10.0 11.6
2020-2024 County Oppland SØ 12.5 13.8
2020-2024 County Hedmark S 12.5 13.2
2020-2024 County Oslo og Akershus 12.5 13.1
2020-2024 County Telemark NV 10.0 11.1
2020-2024 County Nordland N 5.0 5.1
2020-2024 County Finnmark 2.5 2.9

10. Results

The following tables present scaled indicator results at county or sub-county, regional, and national scales for each reporting period. All scaled values are on a 0–1 scale, where 0 corresponds to X0 (0% bilberry cover) and 1 corresponds to the county-specific X100 for counties or sub-counties, or to an area-weighted aggregate of scaled county or sub-county values for regions and the whole country. All three spatial levels use the same underlying 1000 bootstrap draws per county per period (section 9.3), scaled with ec_normalise(). At county level the scaled draws are summarised directly (mean, median, 95% CI, IQR). At regional and national level, ec_upscale() propagates the scaled county draws through the area-weighted spatial hierarchy, also yielding a distribution of 1000 values per unit.

The time series below (Figure 2) summarises regional and national values; the detailed tables follow.

Time series of indicator values by region and period 
bbca_region_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Øst", "2" = "Sør", "3" = "Vest",
  "4" = "Midt", "5" = "Nord", "National" = "National"
)
bbca_region_levels <- c("National", "Midt", "Nord", "Øst", "Sør", "Vest")
bbca_region_cols <- setNames(
  MetBrewer::met.brewer("Archambault", n = length(bbca_region_levels)),
  bbca_region_levels
)

bbca_plot_data <- bbca_mc_combined |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    region_name = dplyr::recode(region, !!!bbca_region_labels),
    series_type = dplyr::if_else(region == "National", "National", "Region")
  ) |>
  dplyr::group_by(period, region_name, series_type) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    median = median(sampled_mean),
    p_025  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.025)),
    p_975  = as.numeric(quantile(sampled_mean, 0.975)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(region_name = factor(region_name, levels = bbca_region_levels))

ggplot2::ggplot(bbca_plot_data,
                ggplot2::aes(x = period, y = median, colour = region_name, group = region_name)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(
    ggplot2::aes(linewidth = series_type),
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.18)
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_pointrange(
    ggplot2::aes(ymin = p_025, ymax = p_975, shape = series_type),
    size = 0.8, linewidth = 0.45,
    position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.18)
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = bbca_region_cols, name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_shape_manual(values = c(National = 19, Region = 17), name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(National = 1, Region = 0.55), guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.box         = "horizontal",
    panel.grid.minor   = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = ggplot2::element_blank()
  ) +
  ggplot2::guides(
    colour = ggplot2::guide_legend(nrow = 2,
                                   override.aes = list(size = 0.7, linewidth = 0.45)),
    shape  = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 0.8, linewidth = 0.45))
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(y = "Indicator value", x = "")

10.1 County-level results (scaled with uncertainty)

10.2 Regional results (scaled with uncertainty)

10.3 National results (scaled with uncertainty)

11. Export file

Regional and national Monte Carlo draws are exported in one table (bbca_mc_combined: year, region, sampled_mean; region contains the five NI region codes and "National"). This mirrors the export format used in the MOOS indicator.

Export regional and national MC-propagated indicator draws 
write.csv(
  bbca_mc_combined,
  here::here("data/results_NO_BBCA_001.csv"),
  row.names = FALSE
)

Study design The study was designed by [AB, CD].

Data collection / fieldwork Field data were collected by [AB, EF] between [years]. Sampling protocol was developed by [CD].

Laboratory procedures DNA extraction was performed by [GH]. Sequencing was conducted by [IJ].

Data processing Raw data were curated and cleaned by [EF]. Code for preprocessing was written by [KL].

Statistical analysis All analyses were conducted by [CD] using [software], with verification by [AB].

Visualization Figures were produced by [KL], with input from [CD].

Reproducibility All code was reviewed and validated by [AB, CD].

TipSession Info

This information is mainly aimed at developers that need to recreate the R environment that produced the html. Important note: this information is about the R session that was running when the html was rendered, and if some of the code chunks were not evaluated when the html was rendered, they may have been executed in a different environment.
